description: Immerse yourself in the vibrant culture and history of Chicago, a city that pulses with energy and creativity. This 5-day solo journey will take you through iconic baseball landmarks, local haunts, and serene spots to unwind, all while keeping your budget in check. From the thrill of catching a game to exploring the rich tapestry of neighborhoods, this trip is designed for the passionate baseball fan and culture enthusiast alike. Get ready to discover the Windy City like never before!

days.title: Arrival in Chicago and Exploring Wrigleyville

activities.description: Start your day with a hearty breakfast at Lou Mitchell's, a Chicago institution known for its delicious pancakes and friendly atmosphere. Don't forget to try their famous coffee and complimentary milk duds!
Head over to Wrigley Field, one of the oldest ballparks in Major League Baseball. Take a guided tour to learn about its rich history and maybe catch a glimpse of the Cubs practicing if you're lucky!
Enjoy lunch at The Cubby Bear, a popular spot right across from Wrigley Field. They offer a variety of American classics and a great atmosphere, especially on game days.
Stroll around Wrigleyville, soaking in the vibrant atmosphere. Check out local shops for Cubs merchandise and unique souvenirs. This area is filled with energy, especially when a game is on!
Take a short trip to the Chicago History Museum to learn about the city's rich past, including its baseball history. The exhibits are engaging and provide a great context for your visit.
Savor a classic Chicago-style hot dog or Italian beef sandwich at Portillo's. This fast-casual restaurant is a must-visit for any foodie in Chicago.
End your day with a peaceful walk at Lakeview Park, enjoying the views of Lake Michigan. It's a perfect spot to unwind and reflect on your day in Chicago.

days.title: Chicago's Baseball Heritage and Local Eats

activities.description: Start your day with a hearty breakfast at Lou Mitchell's, a Chicago institution known for its delicious pancakes and friendly service. Don't forget to try their famous coffee and complimentary donut holes!

Dive into the world of sports at the Chicago Sports Museum, where you can explore interactive exhibits and learn about the city's rich sports history, including its baseball legacy. Perfect for any sports fan!
Enjoy a classic Chicago-style hot dog or Italian beef sandwich at Portillo's. This local favorite offers a casual dining experience with a vibrant atmosphere, perfect for a quick and satisfying meal.

Take a guided tour of Wrigley Field, one of the oldest ballparks in Major League Baseball. Learn about its history, iconic features, and the Chicago Cubs, while soaking in the atmosphere of this legendary venue.

activities.activity: Shopping for Fan Gear at the Cubs Store
activities.description: After the tour, head to the Cubs Store located near Wrigley Field to pick up some official Cubs merchandise. Whether it's a jersey, cap, or other memorabilia, it's the perfect place to find your fan gear!
Stroll through Greektown, known for its rich Greek heritage and delicious cuisine. Take in the vibrant atmosphere and perhaps grab a coffee or dessert at one of the local bakeries.
End your day with a delightful dinner at The Parthenon, a beloved Greek restaurant in Greektown. Enjoy traditional dishes like moussaka or souvlaki in a warm and inviting setting.
After a fulfilling day, return to your hotel to unwind. Take some time to reflect on your day and plan for tomorrow's adventures in Chicago!

Start your day with a hearty breakfast at Lou Mitchell's, a Chicago institution known for its delicious pancakes and friendly service. Don't forget to try their famous coffee and complimentary donut holes!

activities.activity: Visit the Art Institute of Chicago
activities.description: Explore the Art Institute of Chicago, home to an impressive collection of Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces. Take your time wandering through the galleries and don't miss the iconic 'American Gothic' painting.

activities.activity: Lunch at The Art Institute Café
activities.description: Enjoy a light lunch at the café within the Art Institute, where you can savor seasonal dishes made with fresh ingredients while overlooking the beautiful gardens.

After lunch, take a leisurely stroll through Millennium Park. Be sure to snap a photo at the famous Cloud Gate sculpture, affectionately known as 'The Bean', and enjoy the vibrant atmosphere of the park.

activities.activity: Visit the Chicago Cubs Merchandise Store
activities.description: Head to the Chicago Cubs Merchandise Store to pick up some fan gear. Whether you're looking for a jersey, cap, or other memorabilia, this is the perfect place to find your Cubs swag!
Make your way to Navy Pier for a relaxing evening. Enjoy the views of Lake Michigan, take a ride on the Ferris wheel, or simply unwind at one of the many benches along the pier.
Indulge in a classic Chicago deep-dish pizza at Giordano's. This iconic pizzeria is known for its stuffed pizza, and it's a must-try for any visitor to the city.

activities.activity: Evening Walk along the Chicago Riverwalk
activities.description: End your day with a peaceful walk along the Chicago Riverwalk. The illuminated skyline and the gentle sounds of the water create a perfect backdrop for reflection after a fulfilling day.

Start your day with a hearty breakfast at Lou Mitchell's, a Chicago institution known for its delicious pancakes and friendly service. Don't forget to try their famous coffee and complimentary donut holes!
Head to Wrigley Field, one of the oldest ballparks in Major League Baseball. Take a guided tour to learn about its rich history and the Chicago Cubs. The iconic ivy-covered walls are a must-see!
Enjoy a classic Chicago-style hot dog or Italian beef sandwich at Portillo's. This casual eatery is a favorite among locals and visitors alike, offering a taste of Chicago's culinary heritage.
Stroll through the vibrant Pilsen neighborhood, known for its colorful murals and rich Mexican culture. Visit local shops and galleries, and soak in the artistic atmosphere.

activities.activity: Shopping for MLB Merchandise at the Chicago Sports Depot
activities.description: Head to the Chicago Sports Depot to find a wide range of MLB merchandise, including jerseys, caps, and other fan gear. It's the perfect place to pick up some souvenirs!
Indulge in Chicago's famous deep-dish pizza at Giordano's. This iconic pizzeria is known for its stuffed pizza, which is a must-try for any visitor to the city.
End your day with a leisurely walk in Lincoln Park. Enjoy the beautiful gardens and perhaps catch a glimpse of the sunset over the lake. It's a perfect spot to unwind after a busy day.

transit.memo: Direct flight back to Washington D.C.
